results.rendered_html:
1. vīta (von 1. vī) partic. begehrt, beliebt, gern genossen: vītatamāni havyā  ṚV. 7,1,18 . vīte adhvare 9,82,3 . iṣṭaṃ vītamabhigūrtam 1,162,15 . iṣṭaṃ ca vītaṃ cābhūt  ŚĀṄKH. ŚR. 1,14,20 .
— Vgl. vītahavya.

results.rendered_html:
4. vīta (von 3. i mit vi) partic. vergangen, geschwunden, nicht da seiend, fehlend: ˚hiraṇmaya der kein goldenes (Gefäss) besitzt; davon nom. abstr. ˚tva  RAGH. 5,2 . Andere Belege s. u. 3. i mit vi.
— 2〉 abgängig, unbrauchbar; von Pferden und Elephanten  AK. 2,8,2,11 .  TRIK. 3,3,184 .  H. 1252 . an. 2,196 .  MED. t. 58 .
— 3〉 beruhigt (vgl. vītarāga)  H. an.

results.rendered_html:
5. vīta 1〉 adj. schlicht, geradlinig: pṛṣṭheva vītā (= kāntāni  SĀY. ) vṛjinā ca  ṚV. 4,2,11 . stukeva vītā dhanvā vicinvan 9,97,17 .
— 2〉 f. ā Reihe (nebeneinander liegender Gegenstände): darbha˚ (= darbharāji Comm.)  ĀŚV. GṚHY. 4,8,27 .
— Vgl. ˚pṛṣṭha und vīthi.

results.rendered_html:
vīta p. p. [vi˚i-kta]
1 Gone, disappeared.
2 Gone away, departed.
3 Let go, loosed, set free.
4 Excepted, exempt.
5 Approved, liked.
6 Unfit for war.
7 Tame, quiet.
8 Freed from, devoid of (mostly in comp.); vītaciṃta, vītaspṛha, vītabhī, vītaśaṃka &c.
9 Desired, wished for.
10 Put on or worn
— taḥ An elephant or horse unfit or untrained for war.
— taṃ Pricking (an elephant) with the goad and striking with the legs; vītavītabhayā nāgāḥ  Ku. 6. 39  v. l. (see  Malli.  thereon); nirdhūtavītamapi bālakamullalaṃtaṃ  Śi. 5. 47. 
Comp.
— daṃbha a. humble, lowly.
— bhaya a. fearless, intrepid.
— (yaḥ) an epithet of Viṣṇu.
— mala a. pure.
— rāga a.
1 free from desire;  Ku. 6. 39  v. l.
2 free from passion. calm, tranquil.
3 colourless.
— (gaḥ)
1 a sage who has subdued his passions.
2 a deified Jaina saint.
— śokaḥ (= aśokaḥ) the Aśoka tree.
— sūtraṃ the sacred thread.
